title: Forecast Analysis
text:
This chart compares the performance of a sophisticated SARIMA model against a simple Seasonal Naive forecast. The SARIMA model provides a more accurate prediction by capturing complex trends and seasonality in the historical absence data.

forecast_fig.show()

title: Forecast Analysis
text:
Model Performance & Interpretation
mae = forecast_metrics['arima_mae']
naive_mae = forecast_metrics['naive_mae']
pct_better = ((naive_mae - mae) / naive_mae) * 100

print(f"**Forecasting Accuracy (Mean Absolute Error):** The model is currently able to predict daily absence volumes with an average error of **{mae:.1f} absences**. \n")
print(f"This is **{pct_better:.1f}% more accurate** than simply assuming absences will be exactly the same as they were at this time last year (the baseline 'Seasonal Naive' method, which has an average error of {naive_mae:.1f} absences).\n\n")

print("> *Note: A 'Concordance Score' is used to measure the accuracy of Time-to-Event (Survival) models. For time series forecasting like this chart, we use Mean Absolute Error (MAE), which tells us exactly how many people our forecast is off by on an average day.*")
Forecasting Accuracy (Mean Absolute Error): The model is currently able to predict daily absence volumes with an average error of 14.7 absences.
This is 76.3% more accurate than simply assuming absences will be exactly the same as they were at this time last year (the baseline ‘Seasonal Naive’ method, which has an average error of 62.1 absences).

Note: A ‘Concordance Score’ is used to measure the accuracy of Time-to-Event (Survival) models. For time series forecasting like this chart, we use Mean Absolute Error (MAE), which tells us exactly how many people our forecast is off by on an average day.
